page: OVHcloud r2-60 is a memory-optimized virtual server hosted on a KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs powered by an Intel Core Processor based on the Haswell architecture running at 2.2 GHz. The system is equipped with 60.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-core ratio of 15.0 GB per core. Local storage consists of a bundled 100 GB SSD, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate that the r2-60 is positioned for memory-intensive workloads rather than raw computational speed. The CPU performance is modest, with stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks scoring in the bottom 10% and 25% respectively. Similarly, memory bandwidth tests show weak to poor throughput, with large-block read speeds at 19.87 GB/sec and write speeds at 15.43 GB/sec. However, the Passmark memory score achieves an average rating, and the system handles lighter tasks like text generation on a small 135M LLM model at a moderate 80.09 tokens/sec.

Qualitatively, the r2-60 offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy applications that do not require high-performance CPU cycles. The dedicated core allocation ensures predictable, albeit modest, processing power without the risk of noisy-neighbor CPU degradation. This resource density makes it suitable for hosting caching layers, medium-sized databases, and memory-bound background workers where RAM capacity is the primary bottleneck.
description: OVHcloud r2-60 is a memory-optimized virtual server configured with 4 dedicated x86_64 vCPUs and 60.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high 15.0 GB per core allocation. Powered by an Intel Core Processor running at 2.2 GHz on a KVM hypervisor, the instance includes a bundled 100 GB SSD and one public IPv4 address. Benchmark data shows modest CPU capabilities, with single-core and multi-core performance ranking in the lower percentiles, and uncached memory write bandwidth at 15.43 GB/sec. However, its Passmark memory score is average, and it achieves 80.09 tokens/sec on small LLM text generation. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that require a large RAM footprint but have low CPU demands, such as caching servers, in-memory databases, and lightweight data processing pipelines.

KVM hypervisor with dedicated x86_64 architecture
